subject: Math

lesson.story_mission: Help the City Workshop total costs, measure materials, multiply prices, and divide supplies with decimals.
lesson.objective: I can fluently add, subtract, multiply, and divide multi-digit decimals.
lesson.concept_explanation: Decimal operations use place value. Add and subtract by aligning decimal points, multiply then place the decimal using factors, and divide by making the divisor a whole number when needed.
lesson.common_misconception: A common mistake is ignoring place value or placing the decimal point without estimating.
lesson.real_world_connection: Money, metric measurement, sports times, and recipes all use decimal operations.
lesson.lesson_summary: Estimate first, use the correct operation, compute carefully, and compare the result to the estimate.

page_audio.label: Read This Page
page_audio.text: Read This Page. Decimal operations depend on place value. Align decimal points for addition and subtraction. For multiplication and division, estimate first so the decimal point lands in a reasonable place.

worked_examples.text: Add 14.6 and 3.28 by aligning decimal points. The sum is 17.88.
algorithm_steps: Add hundredths, tenths, ones, and tens.
Keep the decimal point aligned in the sum.

page_audio.label: Read This Page
page_audio.text: Read This Page. Watch the decimal addition example. Write fourteen and six tenths as fourteen and sixty hundredths. Align the decimal points, add each place, and the final answer is seventeen and eighty-eight hundredths.

worked_examples.text: Divide 9.6 by 3. The quotient is 3.2 because 3 groups of 3.2 make 9.6.

page_audio.label: Read This Page
page_audio.text: Read This Page. Watch the decimal division example. Nine and six tenths divided by 3 is close to 9 divided by 3, so the answer should be near 3. The exact quotient is three and two tenths.

hint_ladder: Estimate the size of the answer.
Line up place values or rewrite the divisor as a whole number.
Use the operation named by the situation and check the decimal point.

misconception_bank.decimal_alignment_error: Decimal points were not aligned for addition or subtraction.
misconception_bank.decimal_product_size_error: The product decimal point was placed without checking the size of the factors.
misconception_bank.decimal_quotient_error: The divisor or quotient decimal was moved incorrectly.
misconception_bank.operation_choice_error: The situation called for a different operation.

game_theme.title: Decimal City Workshop
game_theme.name: Decimal City Workshop
game_theme.mission_intro: Help the City Workshop total costs, measure materials, multiply prices, and divide supplies with decimals.

story.label: Read This Page
story.text: Read This Page. Welcome to Decimal City Workshop. You will solve mission problems with models, words, and careful checking. Listen for decimal points, place value, products, quotients, and estimates so each answer fits the situation.

lesson.label: Read This Page
lesson.text: Read This Page. This lesson teaches decimal points, place value, products, quotients, and estimates. Read the quantities, name the operation, and connect the symbols to the visual model. A good answer explains what the number means.

watch.label: Read This Page
watch.text: Read This Page. Step one, read the situation and mark the values. Step two, use the model to show the operation. Step three, compute and check whether the final answer is reasonable.

demo.label: Read This Page
demo.text: Read This Page. In the demo, build the model before answering. Say the important math words out loud and notice how each part of the picture matches the equation. Then explain the answer in one sentence.

practice.label: Read This Page
practice.text: Read This Page. Practice with one supported question. Tap Read Question to hear the problem, then use the model and type or choose the answer. Check units, signs, and values before moving on.

challenge.label: Read This Page
challenge.text: Read This Page. The challenge asks you to choose a strategy with less support. Tap Read Question, compare the visual to the equation, and avoid common mistakes before submitting.

checkpoint.label: Read This Page
checkpoint.text: Read This Page. The checkpoint shows what you can do independently. Tap Read Question, solve carefully, and use your answer to prove you understand decimal points, place value, products, quotients, and estimates.

badge.label: Read This Page
badge.text: Read This Page. You completed the Multi-Digit Decimal Operations mission. Celebrate the models, explanations, and accurate answers you used today. The badge marks readiness for more Number System work.

profile.label: Read This Page
profile.text: Read This Page. Your profile saves stars, accuracy, hints, and misconception notes. Use Practice This Skill for focused levels and Mixed review so decimal points, place value, products, quotients, and estimates stay strong.
